claim: The GPT Store's primary value proposition lies in three areas: domain-specific knowledge bases, message thread context management, and API actions/function calling capabilities
actionable: When building GPTs for the store, focus on: uploading custom knowledge bases relevant to specific domains, leveraging conversation history, and integrating meaningful API actions rather than thin doc wrappers
source_title: Is the GPT Store worth your time? High Output Engineering Macro & Micro Discussion

claim: API actions represent 80-90% of the true long-term value of GPTs because they enable connecting to external systems and accomplishing real tasks beyond conversation
actionable: When building GPTs, prioritize designing meaningful custom APIs that your GPT can call to perform domain-specific actions (database queries, system updates, external integrations)
source_title: Is the GPT Store worth your time? High Output Engineering Macro & Micro Discussion

claim: Opening the GPT Store to non-engineers (no coding required) significantly expands the total addressable market but creates a deluge of low-quality GPTs requiring better filtering
actionable: Engineers building GPTs have a competitive advantage in quality; focus on polishing one high-quality GPT rather than competing on quantity, as great ones will stand out from mediocre ones
source_title: Is the GPT Store worth your time? High Output Engineering Macro & Micro Discussion

claim: User-generated content (UGC) on top of AI-generated content (AGC) is OpenAI's strategy to build an app store analogous to Apple's ecosystem
actionable: Understand that GPT Store is positioned as an App Store for AI-powered experiences; focus on creating genuine utility rather than novelty GPTs
source_title: Is the GPT Store worth your time? High Output Engineering Macro & Micro Discussion

claim: Text-to-SQL converters (natural language to database queries) paired with GPTs via API actions represent a promising use case for integrating AI with desktop applications
actionable: Consider building text-to-SQL GPTs that connect to real databases via APIs, allowing users to query data conversationally and having the GPT integrate with desktop apps
source_title: Is the GPT Store worth your time? High Output Engineering Macro & Micro Discussion
